amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: Homologous recombination in E. coli is the primary pathway for repairing double-strand breaks (DSBs), rescuing stalled replication forks, and generating genetic diversity. The RecBCD pathway processes DSB ends: RecBCD helicase-nuclease unwinds and degrades DNA until encountering a Chi sequence, which attenuates nuclease activity and loads RecA onto the 3' overhang. RecA-coated single-stranded DNA (nucleoprotein filament) searches for homologous sequences, invades the homologous duplex forming a D-loop, and catalyzes strand exchange. DNA synthesis extends the invading strand using the homolog as template. The Holliday junction that forms is resolved by RuvABC or RecG to complete recombination. This flowchart illustrates the complete pathway from DSB recognition through RecBCD processing, RecA-mediated strand invasion, branch migration, and junction resolution, including alternative pathways (RecF, RecQ) for different substrates.
mermaid:
graph TD
    Start([Homologous Recombination]) --> A1{DNA substrate type?}
    
    A1 -->|Double-strand break DSB| A2[RecBCD pathway - major pathway]
    A1 -->|Gapped DNA from stalled fork| A3[RecF pathway - alternative]
    A1 -->|Other recombination substrate| A4[RecQ pathway]
    
    A2 --> A5[RecBCD helicase-nuclease binds DSB end]
    A5 --> A6["RecBCD is heterotrimer: RecB RecC RecD"]
    
    A6 --> A7[RecBCD unwinds DNA - dual helicase activity]
    A7 --> A8["RecB helicase: 3' to 5' on one strand"]
    A8 --> A9["RecD helicase: 5' to 3' on other strand faster"]
    
    A9 --> A10[/RecB nuclease degrades 3' ending strand/]
    A10 --> A11[/RecD nuclease degrades 5' ending strand/]
    
    A11 --> A12{Chi sequence encountered?}
    A12 -->|No Chi: 5'-GCTGGTGG-3'| A13[/Nuclease degradation continues/]
    A12 -->|Yes - Chi site recognized| A14[RecC recognizes Chi]
    
    A13 --> A15{DNA fully degraded?}
    A15 -->|Yes| A16[/No recombination - degradation pathway/]
    A15 -->|No - keep scanning| A12
    
    A14 --> A17[RecBCD pauses at Chi]
    A17 --> A18[RecB nuclease activity attenuated]
    A18 --> A19[RecD ejected from complex]
    A19 --> A20[RecBC now loads RecA]
    
    A20 --> A21[3' ssDNA overhang generated downstream of Chi]
    A21 --> A22[RecBC recruits RecA protein]
    
    A22 --> A23{RecA available?}
    A23 -->|Yes| A24[RecA monomers bind ssDNA]
    A23 -->|No| Wait1[Wait for RecA]
    Wait1 --> A23
    
    A24 --> A25[RecA polymerizes on ssDNA 5' to 3']
    A25 --> A26[RecA nucleoprotein filament formed]
    A26 --> A27[~6 nt DNA per RecA monomer right-handed helix]
    
    A3 --> A28[RecFOR pathway at gaps]
    A28 --> A29[RecF RecO RecR load RecA on gaps]
    A29 --> A24
    
    A4 --> A30[RecQ helicase pathway]
    A30 --> A31[RecQ processes various substrates]
    A31 --> A24
    
    A27 --> A32[RecA filament searches for homology]
    A32 --> A33[ATP-bound RecA active for strand exchange]
    
    A33 --> A34{Homologous duplex found?}
    A34 -->|Yes - homology > 50 bp| A35[RecA promotes strand invasion]
    A34 -->|No homology| A36[Search continues or pathway aborted]
    
    A35 --> A37[RecA catalyzes D-loop formation]
    A37 --> A38[Invading 3' strand pairs with complementary strand]
    A38 --> A39[Displaced strand forms D-loop bubble]
    
    A39 --> A40[DNA polymerase III binds 3' end in D-loop]
    A40 --> A41[DNA synthesis extends invading strand]
    A41 --> A42["Template: homologous chromosome or sister"]
    
    A42 --> A43{Second DSB end captured?}
    A43 -->|Yes - double Holliday junction pathway| A44[Second end anneals]
    A43 -->|No - synthesis-dependent strand annealing SDSA| A45[SDSA pathway]
    
    A45 --> A46[Extended strand displaced]
    A46 --> A47[Displaced strand anneals to other DSB end]
    A47 --> A48[Gap filling and ligation]
    A48 --> A49[Non-crossover product]
    
    A44 --> A50[DNA synthesis on both ends]
    A50 --> A51[Ligation creates double Holliday junction dHJ]
    
    A51 --> A52[Two four-way junctions connecting homologs]
    
    A52 --> A53{Which resolution pathway?}
    A53 -->|RuvABC pathway| A54[RuvAB binds Holliday junction]
    A53 -->|RecG pathway| A55[RecG binds and processes junction]
    
    A54 --> A56[RuvA tetramer binds junction center]
    A56 --> A57[RuvB hexameric rings on DNA arms - ATP-driven motor]
    A57 --> A58[Branch migration - junctions move along DNA]
    
    A58 --> A59[RuvC endonuclease recruited]
    A59 --> A60{RuvC cleavage orientation?}
    A60 -->|Horizontal cuts| A61[Crossover products - recombinant]
    A60 -->|Vertical cuts| A62[Non-crossover products - parental]
    
    A55 --> A63[RecG branch migration translocase]
    A63 --> A64[RecG drives branch migration]
    A64 --> A65[Junction regression or fork restoration]
    
    A61 --> A66[Nick sealing by DNA ligase]
    A62 --> A66
    A65 --> A66
    
    A66 --> A67[Recombination complete]
    A67 --> A68[RecA disassembly - ATP hydrolysis]
    A68 --> A69[RecA recycled]
    
    A67 --> A70{Product outcome?}
    A70 -->|Crossover| A71[Genetic exchange between chromosomes]
    A70 -->|Non-crossover| A72[Gene conversion - local sequence change]
    A70 -->|DSB repair| A73[Restored chromosome integrity]
    
    A49 --> A73
    
    A71 --> End1([Recombinant products])
    A72 --> End2([Gene conversion])
    A73 --> End3([Repaired chromosome])
    A16 --> End4[/(DNA degraded)/]
    A36 --> End5([Recombination aborted])

name: Homologous Recombination
notes: Homologous recombination is essential for DSB repair and restart of stalled replication forks. The Chi sequence (5'-GCTGGTGG-3') is a recombination hotspot that regulates RecBCD, occurring about once every 5 kb in the E. coli genome, biased in orientation to protect against degradation from replication origins. RecA is the central recombinase, conserved across all domains (Rad51 in eukaryotes). The choice between crossover and non-crossover products depends on Holliday junction resolution orientation by RuvC. This pathway is homologous to eukaryotic DSB repair and meiotic recombination, making it a paradigm for understanding recombination across life.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on decades of genetic and biochemical characterization of E. coli recombination. All components (RecBCD, RecA, RuvABC, RecG, Chi sites), catalytic mechanisms, and pathway logic have been validated through genetics, biochemistry, and structural studies. The model represents the current understanding of how bacteria use homologous recombination for genome maintenance and the multiple pathway choices available for different DNA substrates.

sources.relevance: Comprehensive review of RecBCD mechanism and Chi sequence regulation

sources.title: The Holliday junction resolvase RuvC is a dimer that acts in trans

sources.relevance: Regulation of recombination by Chi hotspots and RecBCD regulation
sources.title: Control of RecBCD recombination pathways by the Chi sequence
